heading: Program on oil and gas royalties in-kind
label: 15902 Program on oil and gas royalties in-kind

; orsection 1353 of title 43
any other Federal law governing leasing of Federal land for oil and gas development.

children.heading: Satisfaction of royalty obligation
children.label: 1 Satisfaction of royalty obligation
children.text: Delivery by, or on behalf of, the lessee of the royalty amount and quality due under the lease satisfies royalty obligation of the lessee for the amount delivered, except that transportation and processing reimbursements paid to, or deductions claimed by, the lessee shall be subject to review and audit.

children.heading: Definition of marketable condition
children.label: A Definition of marketable condition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “in marketable condition” means sufficiently free from impurities and otherwise in a condition that the royalty production will be accepted by a purchaser under a sales contract typical of the field or area in which the royalty production was produced.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: Royalty production shall be placed in marketable condition by the lessee at no cost to the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: sell or otherwise dispose of any royalty production taken in-kind (other than oil or gas transferred under for not less than the market price; andsection 1353(a)(3) of title 431So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.1

children.label: B
children.text: transport or process (or both) any royalty production taken in-kind.

children.label: A
children.text: transporting the royalty production;

children.label: B
children.text: processing the royalty production;

children.label: C
children.text: disposing of the royalty production; or

children.label: D
children.text: any combination of transporting, processing, and disposing of the royalty production.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary may not use revenues from the sale of oil and gas taken in-kind to pay for personnel, travel, or other administrative costs of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the Secretary may use a portion of the revenues from royalty in-kind sales, without fiscal year limitation, to pay salaries and other administrative costs directly related to the royalty in-kind program.
reimburse the lessee for the reasonable costs of transportation (not including gathering) from the lease to the point of delivery or for processing costs; or
allow the lessee to deduct the transportation or processing costs in reporting and paying royalties in-value for other Federal oil and gas leases.

children.heading: Benefit to the United States required
children.label: d Benefit to the United States required
children.text: The Secretary may receive oil or gas royalties in-kind only if the Secretary determines that receiving royalties in-kind provides benefits to the United States that are greater than or equal to the benefits that are likely to have been received had royalties been taken in-value.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Before making payments under or of revenues derived from the sale of royalty production taken in-kind from a lease, the Secretary shall deduct amounts paid or deducted under subsections (b)(4) and (c) and deposit the amount of the deductions in the miscellaneous receipts of the Treasury.section 191 of title 30section 1337(g) of title 43

children.heading: Accounting for deductions
children.label: 2 Accounting for deductions
children.text: When the Secretary allows the lessee to deduct transportation or processing costs under subsection (c), the Secretary may not reduce any payments to recipients of revenues derived from any other Federal oil and gas lease as a consequence of that deduction.
shall consult with a State before conducting a royalty in-kind program under this part within the State;
may delegate management of any portion of the Federal royalty in-kind program to the State except as otherwise prohibited by Federal law; and

shall consult annually with any State from which Federal oil or gas royalty is being taken in-kind to ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that the royalty in-kind program provides revenues to the State greater than or equal to the revenues likely to have been received had royalties been taken in-value.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: 1 Preference
children.text: If the Secretary finds that sufficient supplies of crude oil are not available in the open market to refineries that do not have their own source of supply for crude oil, the Secretary may grant preference to those refineries in the sale of any royalty oil accruing or reserved to the United States under Federal oil and gas leases issued under any mineral leasing law, for processing or use in those refineries at private sale at not less than the market price.

children.heading: Proration among refineries in production area
children.label: 2 Proration among refineries in production area
children.text: In disposing of oil under this subsection, the Secretary may, at the discretion of the Secretary, prorate the oil among refineries described in paragraph (1) in the area in which the oil is produced.

children.heading: Onshore royalty
children.label: 1 Onshore royalty
children.text: Any royalty oil or gas taken by the Secretary in-kind from onshore oil and gas leases may be sold at not less than the market price to any Federal agency.

children.heading: Offshore royalty
children.label: 2 Offshore royalty
children.text: Any royalty oil or gas taken in-kind from a Federal oil or gas lease on the outer Continental Shelf may be disposed of only under .section 1353 of title 43

children.heading: Federal low-income energy assistance programs
children.label: i Federal low-income energy assistance programs

children.heading: Preference
children.label: 1 Preference
children.text: In disposing of royalty oil or gas taken in-kind under this section, the Secretary may grant a preference to any person, including any Federal or State agency, for the purpose of providing additional resources to any Federal low-income energy assistance program.

children.label: A
children.text: assessing the effectiveness of granting preferences specified in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: providing a specific recommendation on the continuation of authority to grant preferences.
take in-kind royalty gas from any lease on the McAlester Army Ammunition Plant in McAlester, Oklahoma; and

sell such royalty gas to the Department of Defense in accordance with subsection (h)(1), for use only at that plant, only for energy resilience purposes, and only to the extent necessary to meet the natural gas needs of that plant.
